what_it_does: Renders a static 1600x900 presentation-style surface with a centered title, three tinted vertical plates, metric figures, short supporting fragments, faint blueprint corner grids, a striped foot texture, and a circular V mark.
observations: Uses Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and shared CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
The page is centered with body flex layout around a fixed-size .view canvas.
The main composition is three equal grid columns labeled Primary, Support, and Reference.
Tint differentiation is done through warm-neutral plate backgrounds rather than visible dividing lines.
Decorative blueprint grids appear in both top corners with radial mask fades and very low opacity.
The footer is a thin repeating diagonal stripe band, not a large grounding area.
No JavaScript or interactive behavior is present.
standouts: Tone is the organizing device: the three zones use small hue and lightness shifts to establish hierarchy.
The file combines slide-like absolute positioning with tokenized design variables.
The copy explicitly frames the composition as reading by tone rather than by line.
conventions: Uses a local CSS reset at the top of the style block.
Relies on design-token CSS variables for colors, spacing, type sizes, and radius values.
Uses utility-like modifier classes .p1, .p2, and .p3 for zone color differences.
Keeps all component styling inline in the document rather than using a separate file-local stylesheet.
Uses mostly div-based structure for visual layout.
concerns: The fixed 1600x900 canvas and absolute positioning make the layout specific to one slide size.
Root-relative dependency on /surface/styles/vars.css means rendering depends on the hosting context.
Most semantic groupings are divs, so the three plate groups are not exposed as sections or list items.
notes: A fixed slide composition demonstrating three tonal zones on one surface, with subtle blueprint texture and hard-coded presentation copy.
questions: Are the G1 and G7 comments part of a shared design annotation system?
Is the circular V mark a version mark, brand mark, or layout signature?
Should the three plates be read as data categories, narrative zones, or visual hierarchy examples?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose aligns with the file: it presents one surface divided into three zones through tone rather than explicit separating lines.
intention_signals: It seems intended to show how three functional content zones can coexist on one surface while being separated by subtle tonal shifts and restrained supporting decoration.
novelty: The combination of barely visible blueprint corner grids, warm-neutral plate gradients, and explicit tone-over-line messaging gives the composition a measured technical-presentation character.
contribution: Provides a file-local substrate for tonal zoning: three equal content plates, small hue shifts, low-opacity technical texture, centered headline framing, and a restrained footer/mark treatment.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS slide showing three tinted vertical plates on a warm presentation surface with title, metrics, fragment lists, blueprint corner grids, and a thin striped footer.
intention_for_embedding: The file was made to demonstrate a three-zone layout where hierarchy and separation come from tone, warmth, and recessive surfaces rather than hard lines.
